channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Photos: These bold women stand up for justice, rights ... and freedom
item.description: To mark International Women's Day, we feature portraits and profiles of determined women around the world.
item.content:encoded:
To mark International Women's Day, we feature portraits and profiles of determined women around the world.

(Image credit: Annice Lyn)
item.dc:creator: Kamala Thiagarajan

item.title: Israel hits Iran's oil depots as clerics say consensus reached on Ayatollah successor
item.description: Israel targeted Iran's oil facilities for the first time early Sunday, with videos showing huge flames lighting up the sky, while Iranian state media reported officials were close to announcing a successor for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.
item.content:encoded:
Israel targeted Iran's oil facilities for the first time early Sunday, with videos showing huge flames lighting up the sky, while Iranian state media reported officials were close to announcing a successor for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

(Image credit: Vahid Salemi)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Teens are sleeping less than ever and screens aren't primarily to blame
item.description: Nearly a quarter of teens sleep 5 hours or less per night and the majority sleep less than 8 hours. The problem is pervasive and technology doesn't seem to be the main culprit, according to a new report.
item.content:encoded: Nearly a quarter of teens sleep 5 hours or less per night and the majority sleep less than 8 hours. The problem is pervasive and technology doesn't seem to be the main culprit, according to a new report.
item.dc:creator: Ari Daniel

item.title: A new Nepali party, led by an ex-rapper, is set for a landslide win in parliamentary election
item.description: A Nepali political party led by an ex-rapper is set for a landslide victory in the country's first parliamentary election since Gen Z protests ousted the old leadership that has ruled the Himalayan nation for decades.
item.content:encoded:
A Nepali political party led by an ex-rapper is set for a landslide victory in the country's first parliamentary election since Gen Z protests ousted the old leadership that has ruled the Himalayan nation for decades.

(Image credit: Niranjan Shrestha)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: U.S. Judge says Kari Lake broke law in overseeing Voice of America
item.description: He declared all of Lake's actions over the past year to be null and void, including the layoffs of more than 1,000 journalists and staffers.
item.content:encoded:
He declared all of Lake's actions over the past year to be null and void, including the layoffs of more than 1,000 journalists and staffers.

(Image credit: Tom Brenner)
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik

item.title: Trump vows to 'take care of Cuba,' praises Venezuela cooperation at summit
item.description: Trump made the promise in front of an assembled meeting of Latin American leaders.
item.content:encoded:
Trump made the promise in front of an assembled meeting of Latin American leaders.

(Image credit: Rebecca Blackwell)
item.dc:creator: Alana Wise

item.title: British Columbia to make daylight saving time permanent
item.description: The Canadian province is permanently ending the biannual time shifts for more light at the day's end. But research shows daylight saving increases health risks.
item.content:encoded:
The Canadian province is permanently ending the biannual time shifts for more light at the day's end. But research shows daylight saving increases health risks.

(Image credit: Charles Krupa)
item.dc:creator: Rhitu Chatterjee

item.title: How the U.S. and Israel's war with Iran is realigning the politics of the Middle East
item.description: NPR's Adrian Ma speaks with author and journalist Kim Ghattas about the impacts the U.S. and Israel's war with Iran will have on the broader region.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Adrian Ma speaks with author and journalist Kim Ghattas about the impacts the U.S. and Israel's war with Iran will have on the broader region.
item.dc:creator: Michael Levitt

item.title: Shedding light on how Epstein used visits to Interlochen to target girls
item.description: An NPR reporting team sheds new light on how Jeffrey Epstein and Ghislaine Maxwell used their access to the Interlochen Center for the Arts to target girls.
item.content:encoded: An NPR reporting team sheds new light on how Jeffrey Epstein and Ghislaine Maxwell used their access to the Interlochen Center for the Arts to target girls.
item.dc:creator: Ava Berger

item.title: First HBCU D1 women's wrestlers compete at championship
item.description: It's a weekend of firsts in Iowa, where the first national women's college wrestling championship is taking place and the first HBCU Division 1 women's wrestling team is fielding players.
item.content:encoded: It's a weekend of firsts in Iowa, where the first national women's college wrestling championship is taking place and the first HBCU Division 1 women's wrestling team is fielding players.
item.dc:creator: Jason Fuller
